titleStmt.title: Letter from John N Edgeworth to Nicholas Lamb, 20 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J N Edgewoth

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
A letter from John Newcomen Edgeworth to Nicholas Lamb enclosing a copy of the final main roads declaration made at the County Council quarterly meeting.
John Newcomen Edgeworth (b. 1852) was the Secretary to the Longford County Council.
Nicholas Lamb (b. 1861) was the Town Clerk of Longford and served on the Longford Urban District Council. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Longford County Council 20th of May 1916 Dear Sir I enclose herewith copy of Final
Main Boards Declaration made by County Council
of quarterly meeting of Council on the 17th inst. Within seven days of receipt of this notice a
special meeting should be summoned with 14
days notice, to decide whether an appeal shall
be lodged against this declaration. Yours truly J M Edgeworth (Sec) The Clerk Urban Council
Longford.
